extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 3 after timing his sprint perfectly on a cobbled, rising finale with five roundabouts, while UAE's aggressive pre-sprint tactics for Ackermann backfired.
tactical_patterns: UAE burned Pogacar 50km from finish to setup Ackermann sprinter, revealing multi-leader roster confusion under race pressure.
Bonus second hunting by Pogacar with 50km remaining created chase that fatigued rival sprint trains, tactical gambit despite failure.
Late rising cobbled finale with five roundabouts punished early positioning, rewarding sprinters who waited like Ewan versus early launchers.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: hunted intermediate bonus seconds 50km out despite racing for Ackermann, suggests GC ambition overriding sprint leadout duties
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: bridged to Pogacar group on rolling terrain showing Giro-winning climbing form returning
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: faded outside top-5 despite entire UAE train sacrificing itself, questions sprinting form versus tactical execution
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: another podium finish without dedicated leadout shows freakish positioning instincts on technical finales
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: called season disappointing before this stage, pressured enough to launch early and lose to Ewan
